role_summary: Southern Christian Leadership Conference executive director from 1964; close aide to Martin Luther King Jr.; U.S. Representative from Georgia's 5th district (1973-1977); United States Ambassador to the United Nations under President Carter (1977-1979); 55th Mayor of Atlanta (1982-1990).
ai_reading: Young is named in the Clarence B. Jones (entry 20) catalog page as a co-contributor to King's April 4, 1967 "Beyond Vietnam" speech; he is also referenced in the broader SCLC catalog pages (Cotton, Walker, Vivian, Lowery). He is not himself a Civil Rights History Project interviewee. His standalone catalog page is added to anchor the SCLC-second-tier-leadership cohort the catalog's existing SCLC pages discuss. Young's three subsequent careers (Congress, ambassadorship, Atlanta mayoralty) are the cultural-dominant placement; the corpus's SCLC-tier cosines read him as the executive-director-figure of the 1964-1968 inner circle.
biographical_paragraph: Young was born March 12, 1932 in New Orleans to a dentist father and a schoolteacher mother. He earned a divinity degree at Hartford Theological Seminary and served pastorates in Marion, Alabama and Thomasville, Georgia before joining the National Council of Churches in 1957. He moved to SCLC in 1961, becoming its executive director in 1964; in that role he was a chief strategist in the Birmingham, Selma, and St. Augustine campaigns and a key drafter of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 negotiations with the Johnson administration. He was with King in Memphis at the Lorraine Motel on April 4, 1968, returning from the federal court hearing on the second march injunction when King was shot. After King's death he ran successfully for the U.S. House from Atlanta in 1972 and served two terms before President Carter appointed him U.N. Ambassador; he resigned in 1979 over a meeting with a PLO delegate that the Carter administration had not authorized. He served two terms as mayor of Atlanta (1982-1990) and remains active in international development consulting.

sources.title: Andrew Young, An Easy Burden: The Civil Rights Movement and the Transformation of America
